microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's hardware recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing milestones and workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills across enterprise partners like Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable delivery model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive hardware validation capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in **4 months** with a 94% recovery rate, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous-validation release standard.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract, managing risks and mitigation strategies across engineering and partner teams.

Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to reduce **planning cycle time by 39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, freeing engineering bandwidth for higher-priority hardware and software release milestones.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of **AI-driven code generation** workflows, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key engineering teams and saved 37 hours of monthly developer toil.

Influenced the program roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, identifying critical feature gaps and translating findings into prioritized backlog items.

Improved data integrity and retrieval by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting engineer lookup time by 83% across cross-functional teams.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering and operations teams.

Drove a product strategy to mitigate operational toil during hardware and software region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a roadmap to automate critical delivery paths.

Enabled leadership to target automation gaps and prioritize high-impact investments by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil milestones and release dependencies across 140+ cross-functional teams.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping hardware and software dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and managing risks with IT and Engineering stakeholders.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process across cross-functional hardware and software teams.

Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual release overhead across the robotics platform.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end project scoping and invoicing workflows for 200+ monthly proposals, implementing a new CRM process that improved schedule adherence and stakeholder communication.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile to better align messaging with target customer needs.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering actionable recommendations that informed budget and resource planning.

tailoring_notes: 'hardware and software product releases' (JD: '8+ years experience in hardware and software product releases') woven into microsoft_2022 bullet#2 and amazon_robotics bullet#1 to mirror exact JD phrasing.

'risks and mitigation strategies' (JD: 'Manage risks/issues and develop mitigation strategies') used verbatim in microsoft_ft bullet#4 and paraphrased in amazon_robotics bullet#1 to show direct competency match.
'milestones and schedules' (JD: 'develop and manage milestones, schedules and budgets') surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet#1 and microsoft_2022 bullet#3 to align with core TPM delivery language.

'cross-functional' and 'stakeholder' language (JD: 'Interact with diverse technical and non-technical groups') consistently applied across all five roles to reinforce the JD's emphasis on org-wide program execution.

'Agile' framing (JD: 'Expertise in Agile and Waterfall Release Processes') explicitly called out in amazon_robotics bullet#2 and skills section, grounding Cyrus's experience in the JD's required methodology.
